panels.title: Services
panels.description: Shows the total number of distinct services reporting spans in the selected time range. A sudden drop may indicate instrumentation gaps or service outages.

panels.title: Total RPS
panels.description: Shows the aggregate requests per second across all services. A rising value indicates increased traffic; a sudden drop may signal an upstream failure or routing change.

panels.title: Avg Error Rate
panels.description: Shows the mean error percentage averaged across all services. An increase signals degraded reliability and warrants drilling into per-service error rates.

panels.title: Avg p95 Latency
panels.description: Shows the average 95th-percentile latency across all services in milliseconds. Rising values indicate that tail latency is worsening fleet-wide.

panels.title: Request Rate by Service
panels.description: Plots requests per second over time, grouped by service. Watch for sudden spikes that may indicate retry storms or traffic shifts between services.

panels.title: Error Rate by Service
panels.description: Tracks error percentage over time for each service. Diverging lines help isolate which service is the source of a reliability regression.

panels.title: Throughput by Span Kind
panels.description: Shows span volume over time grouped by kind (SERVER, CLIENT, INTERNAL, etc.). Useful for understanding whether traffic growth originates from inbound requests or internal fan-out.

panels.title: Errors by HTTP Route
panels.description: Ranks HTTP routes by total error count to surface the noisiest endpoints. Use this to prioritize which routes need immediate attention.

panels.title: Top Slowest Operations (p99)
panels.description: Lists operations ranked by 99th-percentile duration, showing the worst tail-latency offenders. Focus optimization efforts on the top entries for maximum user-impact improvement.

panels.title: Top Error Operations
panels.description: Lists operations ranked by error rate descending. Rows at the top represent the most unreliable operations and are prime candidates for bug fixes or retry tuning.
